revision.comment: Sync from repo need_translation/ [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24615006798|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|British-born Japan-based writer on Shugendō and the Katsuragi 28 Shuku}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2025}} {{Infobox person | name = Gary Luscombe | other_name = ラスカン ガリー | image = | caption = | nationality = British | residence = Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | occupation = Writer, local guide, hospitality worker | known_for = English-language articles on [[Shugendō]] and the [[Katsuragi 28 Shuku]] (経塚巡拝) | website = }}
'''Gary Luscombe''' (ラスカン ガリー) is a British-born expatriate in Japan. He is known for his writing and research oon [[Shugendō]] (修験道) and the [[Katsuragi 28 Shuku]] (葛城二十八宿) sutra-mound pilgrimage in the mountains of Osaka, Wakayama, and Nara prefectures. He has written articles about the “Kyozuka” (経塚) and on Shugendō festivals and practice in the Kansai region.<ref name="JT28">{{cite web |last=Luscombe |first=Gary |title=Katsuragi 28 Shuku Kyozuka Trail |url=https://en.japantravel.com/osaka/katsuragi-28-shuku-kyozuka-trail/65925 |website=Japan Travel |date=26 July 2022 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref><ref name="OsakaKongo">{{cite web |last=Luscombe |first=Gary |title=Shugendo Rituals and Fire Walking on Mount Kongo |url=https://www.osaka.com/thingstodo/activities/shugendo-rituals-and-fire-walking-on-mount-kongo/ |website=Osaka.com |date=23 January 2024 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref>
==Biography== Luscombe is originally from Guernsey and has lived for years in Osaka Prefecture, based in Kawachinagano in the far south of the prefecture. He works as a receptionist at [https://www.e-oyu.com/ Nanten-en Ryokan] near Amami Station in the Kongo Mountains.<ref name="OsakaAuthor">{{cite web |title=Gary Luscombe (author page) |url=https://www.osaka.com/author/gary_luscombe/ |website=Osaka.com |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Amami Onsen Nanten-en — company page (LinkedIn) |url=https://jp.linkedin.com/company/amami-onsen-nanten-en |website=LinkedIn |language=ja |quote=Lists staff including “Gary Luscombe … Storyteller and Front Desk” |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Gary Luscombe: Crossing Cultures to Work in the Japanese Hospitality Industry |url=https://medium.com/kokoro-media/gary-luscombe-crossing-cultures-to-work-in-the-japanese-hospitality-industry-dfc43b07c5c6 |website=Kokoro Media (Medium) |date=2025 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref>
==Research and writing== Luscombe’s work focuses on documenting Shugendō sites and routes in southern Osaka and adjacent ranges. His articles on the Katsuragi 28 Shuku explain the historical association between the 28 Lotus Sutra chapters and a network of sutra mounds (経塚) said to have been established by [[En no Gyōja]] (役行者).<ref name="JT28" /> He has also written route descriptions through Amami, Nagaretani, and Chihayaguchi highlighting local Shugendō landmarks and access points to the Kyozuka along or beyond the Diamond Trail.<ref>{{cite web |last=Luscombe |first=Gary |title=Off The Beaten Track in Rural Osaka |url=https://www.osaka.com/thingstodo/activities/off-the-beaten-track-rural-osaka/ |website=Osaka.com |date=9 May 2023 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref>
In addition to route research, Luscombe has reported on contemporary Shugendō practice, including the Renge (Lotus) Grand Festival near the summit of Mount Kongō and its ''goma'' (護摩) fire rite and public fire-walking (火渡り).<ref name="OsakaKongo" /> He occasionally leads or assists small walking tours that incorporate Kyozuka sites in Kawachinagano and nearby mountains.<ref>{{cite web |title=Guided walk posts referencing Kyozuka and Katsuragi 28 Shuku (examples) |url=https://www.linkedin.com/posts/gary-luscombe_japan-japanesehistory-japaneseculture-activity-7298920992100298752-jyi8 |website=LinkedIn |date=2024 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Amami village guided walk note |url=https://www.linkedin.com/posts/gary-luscombe_japan-japaneseculture-japanesehistory-activity-7313423900640481280-fJgx |website=LinkedIn |date=2025 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref>
==Katsuragi 28 Shuku== The Katsuragi region (葛城) spans the Izumi and Kongō ranges along the borders of Osaka, Wakayama, and Nara. Official heritage materials describe “Katsuragi Shugen” (葛城修験) as the field in which En no Gyōja first trained, with a pilgrimage that links 28 sutra mounds, waterfalls, megaliths, shrines, and temples associated with Shugendō.<ref>{{cite web |title=A Japan Heritage: The Katsuragi Shugen Story |url=https://katsuragisyugen-nihonisan.com/en/story/ |website=Wakayama Prefectural Government (Japan Heritage) |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=A Japan Heritage: The Katsuragi Shugen Story (site top) |url=https://katsuragisyugen-nihonisan.com/en/ |website=Wakayama Prefectural Government (Japan Heritage) |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref> Related exhibits and research have highlighted the role of Shippōryū-ji (七宝瀧寺) and medieval figures in the development of the Katsuragi pilgrimage network.<ref>{{cite web |title=2019 Special Exhibition “Shipporyuji and the monk Shiichi — The world of the Katsuragi Shugendo 28 Shuku” |url=https://www.wakayama-u.ac.jp/kisyuken/event/2019102400029/ |website=Institute of Kishu Economic and Cultural History, Wakayama University |language=ja |date=24 October 2019 |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref> Shippōryū-ji identifies itself as a head temple and training center for the “Katsuragi 28 Shuku” tradition.<ref>{{cite web |title=Shipporyuji (Inunakisan) official site |url=https://inunakisan.jp/ |website=Shipporyuji |language=ja |access-date=17 August 2025}}</ref>
==Personal life== Luscombe lives near Amami Station with his wife and children.
==Selected articles== *{{cite web |last=Luscombe |first=Gary |title=Katsuragi 28 Shuku Kyozuka Trail |url=https://en.japantravel.com/osaka/katsuragi-28-shuku-kyozuka-trail/65925 |website=Japan Travel |date=26 July 2022}} (English guide to the route and several Kyozuka in Kawachinagano) *{{cite web |last=Luscombe |first=Gary |title=Shugendo Rituals and Fire Walking on Mount Kongo |url=https://www.osaka.com/thingstodo/activities/shugendo-rituals-and-fire-walking-on-mount-kongo/ |website=Osaka.com |date=23 January 2024}} *{{cite web |last=Luscombe |first=Gary |title=Off The Beaten Track in Rural Osaka |url=https://www.osaka.com/thingstodo/activities/off-the-beaten-track-rural-osaka/ |website=Osaka.com |date=9 May 2023}}
==See also== *[[Shugendō]] *[[En no Gyōja]] *[[Katsuragi 28 Shuku]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://en.japantravel.com/profile/gary-luscombe/807584741 Contributor profile at ''Japan Travel''] *[https://www.osaka.com/author/gary_luscombe/ Author page at ''Osaka.com'']
[[Category:Living people]] [[Category:British expatriates in Japan]] [[Category:British writers]] [[Category:People from Guernsey]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[en:Gary Luscombe]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]] [[Category:Need translation]]
